question: A vending machine usually provides snack including soft drink, coffee and cookies, on insertion of a coin. Over a period of time, it is found not to work on three uses in every thousand times on the average. If we define $X$ as a random variable and that $x=1$ if the machine works and $x=0$ if it fails to work.
answer:
Thus, we obtain

$$
P(X=x)= \begin{cases}0.997 & \text { for } x=1 \\ 0.003 & \text { for } x=0\end{cases}
$$
